parsed_review.summary: CP00-172 adds descriptor-only operational-edge failure taxonomy artifacts (catalog, per-failure descriptors, validators, Hermes/Claude/handoff packet builders). Code is side-effect free: no real-data loading, no runtime permission/audit mutation, no Hermes emission or command execution, no Claude prompt send or review execution, no route dispatch, no retry/rollback/compensation execution, no runtime lock acquisition, no case-note writes; customer-facing operational summaries are limited to safe codes/booleans and the leak-guard internal_reference_fields/prohibited_outputs checks hold; HRX stays embedded and LDIP is not implemented. Contract section matches the registry catalog under the order-sensitive JSON compare. Approval is blocked by one P1: the contract no_write_attestation omits builds_blocked_claim_receipt_descriptor and builds_failure_fixture_descriptor that the registry attestation declares, which makes scripts/validate-rp04-master-data-contract.mjs fail. Fix the contract drift and re-validate; a P3 notes that coverage validity relies on plan/manifest data outside this diff.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-172 builds frozen operational edge descriptors only; package code does not load real data, execute runtime permission checks, audit appends, route dispatch, API handlers, UI rendering, AI retrieval, analytics, Hermes, Claude, retry, rollback, compensation, locks, or case-note writes.

findings.title: no_write_attestation drift: contract omits two CP172 build flags
findings.description: MASTER_DATA_CP172_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION in packages/master-data/src/registry.js declares eleven new build flags including builds_blocked_claim_receipt_descriptor: true and builds_failure_fixture_descriptor: true. The contract's no_write_attestation block only adds nine of them (through builds_compensation_expectation_descriptor) and omits builds_blocked_claim_receipt_descriptor and builds_failure_fixture_descriptor. scripts/validate-rp04-master-data-contract.mjs iterates Object.entries(MASTER_DATA_CP172_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION) and requires every key to exist with an equal value in contract.no_write_attestation, so it will push 'no_write_attestation.builds_blocked_claim_receipt_descriptor is required' and 'no_write_attestation.builds_failure_fixture_descriptor is required' (plus the requireEqual undefined!==true mismatches) and exit non-zero. The pack therefore fails its own contract validation gate. Note model.test.js still passes because validateMasterDataCp172FailureTaxonomyOperationalEdgeBoundary does not deep-compare the no_write_attestation against the contract; only the standalone script enforces it.
findings.required_action: Add "builds_blocked_claim_receipt_descriptor": true and "builds_failure_fixture_descriptor": true to contracts/master-data-contract.json no_write_attestation so it matches MASTER_DATA_CP172_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, then re-run scripts/validate-rp04-master-data-contract.mjs to confirm it passes.
findings.post_review_disposition: rejected_as_factually_resolved_and_code_adjusted: contract line 296-297 already contains builds_blocked_claim_receipt_descriptor and builds_failure_fixture_descriptor with true values, npm run rp04:master-data:validate passes, and CP172 duplicate redeclarations were removed from the registry to prevent diff-only ambiguity while preserving inherited true attestation values.

findings.title: Coverage/Hermes/Claude packet validity depends on external plan data not in diff
findings.description: validateMasterDataCp172Coverage (and the Hermes/Claude packets that call it) require closeout-pack-plan.json to contain a CP00-172 pack (or docs/closeout-packs/cp00-172/manifest.json plan_binding_snapshot) whose included_units match deliverable_distribution {failure_recovery:6, security_audit:1, implementation:1, hermes_evidence:1, fixture:1}, micro_phase {RP04.P07.M06:10}, and the ten title_distribution entries. None of these data files are present in the diff, so the green coverage result and coverage_valid:true assertions could not be independently confirmed from the diff alone.
findings.required_action: Confirm closeout-pack-plan.json (or the CP00-172 manifest fallback) enumerates CP00-172 with the exact unit/deliverable/micro-phase/title distribution before relying on the coverage and evidence-packet results.
findings.post_review_disposition: confirmed_by_evidence: docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json contains the CP00-172 plan binding and npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate passed before artifact generation.
